query: We have a binary classifier that flags malformed rows in our ETL pipeline — input is a database row, output is malformed/well-formed. Audit it for fairness.
expected_behavior: Identifies that the prediction subject is a data row, not a person, and that no protected-attribute audit applies in the standard sense
Does NOT force the demographic-parity / equal-opportunity workflow onto this classifier

Suggests handing off to a general performance evaluation skill (e.g., evaluating-binary-classifiers) and/or notes that fairness becomes relevant only if downstream effects of misclassification disparately impact people, in which case the question moves to that downstream system
